test/notification.purpose: Integration test suite for the notification domain: channels, rules, events, delivery status, webhook delivery via Svix, and balance-threshold consumer handler. Wires the full notification stack including a real Svix client from environment variables via TestEnv.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for service access
patterns.description: All test suites receive a TestEnv value and call env.Notification(), env.NotificationRepo(), env.Feature(), env.Meter(), env.NotificationWebhook() instead of accessing service structs directly. TestEnv is created via NewTestEnv(t, ctx, namespace) and must be closed via env.Close().
patterns.example: env, err := NewTestEnv(t, ctx, namespace); service := env.Notification()

patterns.name: Sub-suite structs with Setup + named test methods
patterns.description: Test behaviour is split into XxxTestSuite structs (ChannelTestSuite, RuleTestSuite, EventTestSuite, RepositoryTestSuite, WebhookTestSuite, BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite) each with an optional Setup method. The top-level TestNotification function constructs, sets up, and runs subtests.
patterns.example: testSuite := ChannelTestSuite{Env: env}; t.Run("Create", func(t *testing.T) { testSuite.TestCreate(ctx, t) })

patterns.name: Svix from SVIX_HOST env var
patterns.description: testenv.go reads SVIX_HOST (defaults to 127.0.0.1) and SVIX_JWT_SECRET to connect to a real Svix instance. Webhook delivery assertions require docker compose up -d svix or SVIX_HOST set.

patterns.name: NewBalanceSnapshotEvent for balance consumer tests
patterns.description: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite uses NewBalanceSnapshotEvent(BalanceSnapshotEventInput{Feature, Value, Namespace}) to construct synthetic snapshot.SnapshotEvent values passed to handler.Handle(ctx, event).
patterns.example: snapshotEvent := NewBalanceSnapshotEvent(BalanceSnapshotEventInput{Feature: s.feature, Value: snapshot.EntitlementValue{Balance: convert.ToPointer(50.0)}, Namespace: s.namespace})

patterns.name: setupNamespace for isolated balance handler tests
patterns.description: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite.setupNamespace creates a fresh ULID namespace, replaces meters, creates feature/channel/rule, and constructs consumer.EntitlementSnapshotHandler. Called inside each TestXxx method, not a shared Setup.

patterns.name: eventHandler started as goroutine, closed via env.Close()
patterns.description: testenv.go starts eventHandler.Start() in a goroutine inside NewTestEnv. The closerFunc calls eventHandler.Close() then closes Ent and PG drivers. Always defer env.Close() or use t.Cleanup.

key_file_guides.role: Boots the full notification stack: Ent adapter, Svix webhook handler (real Svix client), pglockx lock client, notification event handler goroutine, notification service, feature connector, and meter mock adapter. Returns closerFunc for cleanup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: eventHandler.Start() runs in a goroutine — must call env.Close(). Svix connectivity is required for webhook delivery tests. TestEnv fields are set only after NewTestEnv returns.

key_file_guides.role: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite and helpers. Tests multi-step threshold crossing and reset deduplication logic of the balance consumer handler via direct handler.Handle calls (not Kafka).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestEntitlementCurrentUsagePeriod is a package-level var computed at init (time.Now()); tests depending on exact period boundaries may be flaky near boundaries.

key_file_guides.role: Top-level TestNotification entry point: creates one TestEnv, constructs all sub-suites, calls Setup on those that need it, runs subtests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All sub-suites share the same TestEnv and namespace — test data created in one suite is visible to others. Use distinct IDs/names.

key_file_guides.role: RepositoryTestSuite tests repo-layer filter methods (ListEvents with Features/Subjects filters). Calls repo directly via env.NotificationRepo() to bypass service layer for precise filter testing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in TestFilterEventByFeature/TestFilterEventBySubject — acceptable here since these are pure repo-filter tests without Ent tx propagation needs.

key_file_guides.role: Stateless helpers: NewSvixAuthToken (generates HS256 JWT for Svix API auth) and NewClickhouseClient (present but currently unused by notification tests).
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewClickhouseClient exists for potential future use; notification tests do not currently use ClickHouse directly.

anti_patterns: Running webhook delivery assertions without a live Svix instance — tests fail if SVIX_HOST is unset and assertions hit Svix API.
Creating a second TestEnv inside a test method — each TestEnv starts a goroutine and opens DB connections; always reuse the suite-level env.
Forgetting defer env.Close() — leaves the eventHandler goroutine and DB/Ent connections open.
Using package-level time vars (TestEntitlementCurrentUsagePeriod) as exact assertion values in time-sensitive tests — value is computed at package init.
Calling env.Notification() or env.NotificationRepo() before NewTestEnv completes — testEnv fields are only set after NewTestEnv returns.

decisions.decision: TestEnv interface rather than exposing service structs directly to test suites.
decisions.rationale: Decouples suites from concrete wiring in testenv.go; allows swapping Svix for a mock without modifying every test file.

decisions.decision: Wire a real Svix client (not a mock) in testenv.go.
decisions.rationale: Notification correctness depends on Svix application/endpoint/message creation semantics that a mock cannot catch; gated by SVIX_HOST availability.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new balance-consumer test step to BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite

test/subscription.purpose: Integration test suite for subscription lifecycle scenarios — verifying the full billing+sync stack (plan creation, subscription create/edit/cancel, billing sync, invoice line generation) against a real PostgreSQL database via pgtestdb. Tests are scenario-based and exercise cross-domain flows that unit tests cannot cover.

patterns.name: Single shared setup() function via testDeps
patterns.description: All tests call setup(t, setupConfig{}) which wires every dependency from scratch using direct constructors (no app/common imports). The returned testDeps struct embeds subscriptiontestutils.SubscriptionDependencies and adds billing, sync, and app services. Always defer tDeps.cleanup(t).

patterns.name: clock.SetTime for deterministic time control
patterns.description: Tests set a fixed start time via testutils.GetRFC3339Time then advance with clock.SetTime(currentTime.Add(...)). All services use pkg/clock so this controls all time-dependent billing logic.

patterns.name: Numbered step comments for scenario readability
patterns.description: Test bodies use numbered comments (// 1st, // 2nd, etc.) documenting the causal sequence: feature → plan → publish → customer → subscription → mutations → assertions.
patterns.example: // 1st, let's create the features ... // 2nd, let's create the plan ... // 4th, let's create the subscription

patterns.name: Plan published before subscription creation
patterns.description: Every scenario calls PlanService.PublishPlan with EffectiveFrom before creating a subscription via pcSubscriptionService.Create. An unpublished plan causes subscription creation to fail.
patterns.example: p, err = tDeps.PlanService.PublishPlan(ctx, plan.PublishPlanInput{NamespacedID: p.NamespacedID, EffectivePeriod: productcatalog.EffectivePeriod{EffectiveFrom: lo.ToPtr(currentTime)}})

patterns.name: minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate for billing profile setup
patterns.description: Tests that exercise billing call minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate(tDeps.sandboxApp.GetID()) (defined in framework_test.go) to get a baseline billing profile with AlignmentKindSubscription and PT0S collection interval. Override fields before calling billingService.CreateProfile.

patterns.name: Hardcoded test-namespace
patterns.description: All tests use namespace := "test-namespace" — hardcoded in subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps. Do not use any other namespace string.

patterns.name: Direct constructor wiring, no app/common imports
patterns.description: framework_test.go builds billingservice, billingadapter, subscriptionsyncservice, appservice, etc. by calling their New(Config{...}) constructors directly. Importing app/common creates import cycles.

key_file_guides.role: Defines testDeps struct, setup() function, and minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate. All scenario files share this single test package (package subscription_test). Registers appsandbox.NewMockableFactory as side-effect before billing service use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new service to setup() requires matching field in testDeps; forgetting cleanup(t) leaks the pgtestdb database; sandbox app must be registered via appsandbox.NewMockableFactory before billing wiring.

key_file_guides.role: Tests that editing a running subscription preserves the metered entitlement's current usage period boundaries after a PatchRemoveItem + PatchAddItem.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Assertions compare sView.Phases[0].ItemsByKey[key][0] (old/expired) vs sUpdated.Phases[0].ItemsByKey[key][1] (new replacement) — index 1 is the replacement entitlement.

key_file_guides.role: Tests editing then immediately canceling a subscription; also stress-tests with 10 parallel customers to surface concurrency issues.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extra customers use unique subject keys (subject_2..subject_11); ensure all subject keys are unique per customer or customer service rejects duplicates.

key_file_guides.role: Regression test for subscription creation with a metered entitlement in the second phase (not the first). Does NOT create a billing profile — tests subscription correctness only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: 'THIS IS THE TEST, it used to fail' comment marks the key assertion. Do not add billing assertions here without first creating a profile.

key_file_guides.role: Tests billing anchor alignment: in-arrears usage, in-advance flat fee, and daily cadence ratecards align to first-of-month. Calls subscriptionSyncService.SynchronizeSubscription directly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Line count depends on days between startOfSub and endOfMonth. Assertions group lines via lo.GroupBy and sort by period start — fragile to date changes in GetRFC3339Time values.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set — creates import cycles with domain testutils packages.
Using context.Background() where t.Context() is available — test harness lifecycle must own context cancellation.
Using a namespace other than 'test-namespace' — hardcoded in SetupDBDeps infrastructure.
Calling subscriptionSyncService or billingService without first creating a billing profile — profile is required for invoice generation.
Mutating clock.Now() via clock.SetTime without restoring it after the test — leaked global clock state affects parallel tests.

decisions.decision: Scenario files rather than table-driven tests.
decisions.rationale: Each scenario exercises a different causal sequence of domain operations varying in setup, timing, and assertions; table-driven structure would obscure the temporal flow central to billing correctness.

decisions.decision: All wiring done via direct constructors in framework_test.go instead of app/common Wire sets.
decisions.rationale: Avoids import cycles between domain testutils and the application wiring layer; keeps test dependencies minimal and explicit.

decisions.decision: Shared testDeps with embedded SubscriptionDependencies rather than per-test setup structs.
decisions.rationale: Each test calls setup() independently (fresh pgtestdb DB), but the struct shape is shared so scenario files can access all services without re-declaring them.

code_examples.scenario: Standard scenario: setup deps, control clock, create feature+plan+customer, subscribe, advance time, assert
